pick.rationale: Wisconsin is significantly stronger than High Point according to advanced metrics, ranking 21st–22nd nationally with elite offensive efficiency (top 11) and a solid defense (top 50), while High Point ranks 92nd–93rd with merely above-average offense and a subpar defense (148th). Although High Point boasts an impressive 30–4 record, it came against the weaker Big South schedule, whereas Wisconsin competed in the tough Big Ten and demonstrated high-level performance throughout the season. Without any reported injuries or availability concerns for Wisconsin, the Badgers’ superior talent, efficiency, and experience make them a clear favorite in this 5/12 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Wisconsin vs High Point Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated team strength using Bart Torvik and KenPom ratings; confirmed no major injury concerns via attempted news searches; assessed schedule context.
evidence: Wisconsin: Torvik rank 21 (adjO 8th, adjD 48th), KenPom rank 22 (adjO 11th, adjD ~45th)
High Point: Torvik rank 93 (adjO 73rd, adjD 148th), KenPom rank 92 (adjO 66th, adjD ~150th)
Wisconsin record: 24–10 (Big Ten); High Point record: 30–4 (Big South)
No injury reports found for either team despite search attempts

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
